---
title: "aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0 — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [7, 23, 16, 16, 20, 18]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-10-vs-day-0 amino-acid Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control) and columns are ribosome sites (A-site / P-site / E-site, left to right). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against log2(odds ratio) on the x-axis (x range about -0.5 to +0.5); each glyph is an amino-acid one-letter code coloured by physicochemical class (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is log2(OR) = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.5, and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 20. Positive log2(OR) means day-10-enriched relative to day-0 within the same condition; negative means day-10-depleted. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is read by comparing the paired top (BWM) and bottom (control) rows of the grid panel by panel.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), within-condition day-10-vs-day-0 contrast per (condition, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, amino acid) 2x2 day-10-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 20 amino acids (120 tests total). The test is within-condition: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency. Effect column is `log2_OR` = log2 of the odds ratio (positive = day-10-enriched relative to day-0, negative = depleted).

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed before Fisher's exact; the source script literally prints a pseudoreplication warning. Anti-conservative p-values.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (1e5-1e6); Fisher's exact returns vanishing or exactly-zero p-values for tiny absolute deviations. The odds ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is per (condition, site) within each contrast file (~20 aa or ~61 codon tests per family); `p_adj` means "corrected within this slice", not across the whole CSV.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition and between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, these tests do not mix conditions or timepoints; each Fisher is held within one (condition, contrast) cell. Structurally cleaner on the pooling-as-confound axis; a positive design feature.

---

## Headline

At AA-level within-condition Fisher d10 vs d0, 104/120 tests clear FDR<0.05 (BWM 54/60, control 50/60): 46 site x aa cells are significant in both conditions, 8 in BWM only, 4 in control only. Minimum adjusted p underflows to 0.0 (4 cells) at whole-transcriptome pooled N (totals 1.3M-3.4M), so p magnitude is uninformative here and the informative axis is `effect change` (BWM `log2_OR` - control `log2_OR`); effects are small-to-moderate.

The largest BWM-vs-control divergences concentrate at site A: A:W (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.586 depleted at d10 vs control +0.088 enriched), A:N (BWM +0.250 vs control -0.129), A:Y (BWM -0.262 vs control +0.017), A:L (BWM -0.182 vs control +0.044), A:I (BWM +0.209 vs control -0.017) — each a direction split between conditions. Reported at equal billing, the largest shared-direction cells (both arms same sign) are E:P both depleted (BWM -0.387 / control -0.086), P:G both depleted (-0.225 / -0.052), E:S both enriched (+0.246 / +0.118), P:E both enriched (+0.208 / +0.101), A:M both enriched (+0.207 / +0.096).

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (log2 of the Fisher odds ratio for the day-10-vs-day-0 contrast within one condition; positive = day-10-enriched relative to day-0, negative = day-10-depleted). Each row pairs the BWM and control `log2_OR` for one (site, amino acid) cell. The `effect change` column is BWM `log2_OR` minus control `log2_OR`: near zero means both conditions moved together (a shared timecourse drift), large magnitude means the two conditions' day-10-vs-day-0 trajectories diverge — positive when BWM rose relative to control, negative when control rose relative to BWM. The three sections split every cell significant at FDR<0.05 in at least one condition by which condition(s) reach significance: both, BWM only, control only; cells significant in neither are omitted. Because the pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts almost every cell clears FDR<0.05 and p magnitude is uninformative, so significance is carried by section membership rather than a displayed p column, and ranking is by `|effect change|`, not by p. Within each section rows are grouped by `site` in A / P / E order, then sorted by `effect change` descending (no row cap). Each amino-acid one-letter code is expanded to `<letter> (<full name>)` on first appearance in each section.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in both conditions

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | N (asparagine) | +0.250 | -0.129 | +0.379 |  |
| A | I (isoleucine) | +0.209 | -0.017 | +0.226 |  |
| A | V (valine) | +0.089 | -0.057 | +0.146 |  |
| A | M (methionine) | +0.207 | +0.096 | +0.111 |  |
| A | Q (glutamine) | -0.103 | -0.179 | +0.077 |  |
| A | K (lysine) | -0.083 | -0.146 | +0.063 |  |
| A | E (glutamate) | +0.154 | +0.097 | +0.057 |  |
| A | F (phenylalanine) | +0.107 | +0.094 | +0.013 |  |
| A | S (serine) | +0.063 | +0.083 | -0.020 |  |
| A | G (glycine) | -0.095 | -0.063 | -0.032 |  |
| A | H (histidine) | -0.094 | -0.038 | -0.056 |  |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.098 | +0.038 | -0.136 |  |
| A | C (cysteine) | -0.046 | +0.164 | -0.211 |  |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.182 | +0.044 | -0.226 |  |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | -0.262 | +0.017 | -0.279 |  |
| A | W (tryptophan) | -0.586 | +0.088 | -0.674 |  |
| P | L | +0.093 | -0.104 | +0.197 |  |
| P | M | +0.103 | -0.066 | +0.170 |  |
| P | W | +0.035 | -0.107 | +0.141 |  |
| P | E | +0.208 | +0.101 | +0.106 |  |
| P | V | +0.014 | -0.052 | +0.065 |  |
| P | I | -0.084 | -0.137 | +0.054 |  |
| P | K | -0.119 | -0.171 | +0.052 |  |
| P | S | +0.084 | +0.044 | +0.040 |  |
| P | P (proline) | -0.024 | -0.045 | +0.022 |  |
| P | Q | -0.032 | -0.044 | +0.012 |  |
| P | H | -0.034 | -0.042 | +0.008 |  |
| P | Y | -0.067 | -0.026 | -0.042 |  |
| P | A | -0.014 | +0.034 | -0.048 |  |
| P | C | +0.112 | +0.175 | -0.064 |  |
| P | R (arginine) | -0.034 | +0.055 | -0.090 |  |
| P | D (aspartate) | +0.061 | +0.223 | -0.162 |  |
| P | G | -0.225 | -0.052 | -0.173 |  |
| E | S | +0.246 | +0.118 | +0.128 |  |
| E | M | +0.134 | +0.053 | +0.082 |  |
| E | G | -0.069 | -0.133 | +0.064 |  |
| E | C | +0.110 | +0.052 | +0.058 |  |
| E | Y | -0.050 | -0.083 | +0.033 |  |
| E | E | +0.072 | +0.046 | +0.026 |  |
| E | L | +0.093 | +0.076 | +0.017 |  |
| E | D | +0.066 | +0.054 | +0.013 |  |
| E | T (threonine) | +0.031 | +0.021 | +0.010 |  |
| E | V | -0.038 | -0.017 | -0.022 |  |
| E | I | -0.070 | -0.034 | -0.036 |  |
| E | K | -0.129 | -0.066 | -0.063 |  |
| E | P | -0.387 | -0.086 | -0.302 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in BWM only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | D (aspartate) | +0.229 | +0.003 | +0.226 |  |
| A | P (proline) | -0.231 | -0.003 | -0.228 |  |
| P | T (threonine) | +0.044 | +0.008 | +0.037 |  |
| E | H (histidine) | +0.125 | -0.020 | +0.145 |  |
| E | W (tryptophan) | +0.083 | -0.001 | +0.084 |  |
| E | Q (glutamine) | +0.076 | +0.001 | +0.075 |  |
| E | N (asparagine) | +0.018 | +0.009 | +0.009 |  |
| E | R (arginine) | -0.015 | +0.007 | -0.021 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in control only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | R (arginine) | +0.007 | +0.073 | -0.066 |  |
| P | F (phenylalanine) | +0.011 | +0.050 | -0.039 |  |
| P | N (asparagine) | -0.013 | +0.083 | -0.096 |  |
| E | A (alanine) | -0.010 | +0.015 | -0.024 |  |

### Flag glossary

No rows are flagged at amino-acid resolution: every (condition, site, amino acid) cell has both day-10 and day-0 counts well above the test's stability threshold (the smallest arm is BWM E:W with day-10 = 15966). The `low-count` flag that the codon sister CSV carries (where the in-frame stop TGA trips it) does not fire here.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 120 (60 per condition; 3 sites x 20 AAs each)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 104 (BWM 54/60, control 50/60)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 108
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow; 4 cells at exactly 0.0). Smallest non-zero p_adj is 2.087e-318 (BWM, E, S).
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the millions has no meaningful floor; the dominant statistical-design concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide).
- Per (condition, site) sig at FDR<0.05: BWM A 18/20, P 18/20, E 18/20; control A 17/20, P 19/20, E 14/20.
- Section split: 46 cells sig in both conditions, 8 BWM-only, 4 control-only (58 of 60 site x aa cells sig in >=1 condition).

## Biological interpretation

**At whole-transcriptome read depth almost every cell is statistically significant, so effect size, not p-value, is the axis that carries information (concrete).** 104 of 120 (condition, site, amino acid) tests clear FDR<0.05 and four cells underflow to an adjusted p of exactly 0.0. With pooled day-10 + day-0 footprint totals in the millions (1.3M-3.4M per arm), Fisher's exact returns vanishing p-values for even tiny relative shifts, so "significant in both / BWM only / control only" is a weak way to rank cells. The quantity that does carry information is `effect change` — how far apart the two conditions' day-10-vs-day-0 trajectories sit — and those magnitudes are small-to-moderate (the largest is A:W at -0.674).

**The two conditions' day-10-vs-day-0 trajectories diverge most at the A site (concrete).** The largest-magnitude `effect change` cells all sit at the A site and all are direction splits: A:W (BWM depleted -0.586 at day 10 vs control enriched +0.088), A:Y (-0.262 vs +0.017), A:L (-0.182 vs +0.044), A:N (+0.250 vs -0.129), A:I (+0.209 vs -0.017), and A:C (-0.046 vs +0.164). In each case one condition moves one way across the timecourse while the other moves the opposite way or stays flat. Because each arm is tested only against its own day-0 baseline, these splits are the design-target reading of a within-condition contrast: they describe genuinely different A-site occupancy trajectories in the perturbed (BWM) and unperturbed (control) lines, not a single shared drift. The A site is the decoding and accommodation position, so a divergence concentrated there is consistent with the two conditions sampling different A-site residue (and therefore codon) landscapes by day 10; whether that reflects altered decoding kinetics or shifted transcript composition is not resolvable from this file alone.

**A separate set of cells moves the same way in both conditions, marking timecourse drift that is not condition-specific (concrete).** The largest shared-direction cells are E:P depleted in both arms (BWM -0.387 / control -0.086), P:G depleted in both (-0.225 / -0.052), E:S enriched in both (+0.246 / +0.118), P:E enriched in both (+0.208 / +0.101), and A:M enriched in both (+0.207 / +0.096). Their small `effect change` is the signature of a shared trajectory rather than a divergence. Shared-direction cells are the least-confounded reading of a within-condition contrast: they describe how each line's day-10 occupancy differs from its own day-0 baseline in a way the two lines agree on, so the BWM perturbation is unlikely to be what drives them. The most parsimonious anchor is that, across the 10-day window, ribosomes sample a slightly shifted residue distribution at each site (more Ser and less Pro at the E site, more Glu and less Gly at the P site) independent of the BWM/control axis.

**Whether the A-site divergences are amino-acid-level or driven by single synonymous codons is the open question for the codon sister (exploratory).** The site-A splits (A:W, A:N, A:Y, A:L, A:I) are reported here at amino-acid resolution, which aggregates each residue's synonymous codons. A divergence that is genuine at the residue level should persist when the same contrast is read at codon resolution; one that collapses onto a single synonym (for example one of leucine's six codons) points to a codon-usage signature rather than residue-level biology. This is a hypothesis to test against `codon_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0`, not a finding from this file — directional, not established here.

**Whether the day-10 picture is already present at day 5, and whether the shared cells are merely stable baselines, are follow-up checks rather than conclusions (exploratory).** This file compares day 10 to day 0; it cannot say whether a divergence emerges gradually or appears abruptly in the second half of the timecourse. Reading it alongside `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0` and `aa_timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5` would show whether the A-site splits accumulate monotonically or are biphasic. Separately, the shared-direction cells (E:P, E:S, P:E, A:M) should be checked against `within_condition_binomial`: a cell that is a stable baseline across all six condition-by-timepoint groups is a standing compositional feature both lines carry, not a day-10-vs-day-0 effect. Both are cross-checks to carry forward, not claims this file establishes.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the near-universal significance at whole-transcriptome N (read by `effect change`, not p); the A-site BWM-vs-control direction splits (A:W, A:Y, A:N, A:L, A:I, A:C) as the design-clean divergence signal; and the shared-direction E:P / E:S / P:E / A:M / P:G cells as condition-independent timecourse drift. *Exploratory*: whether the A-site splits localise to single synonymous codons (the codon sister), whether they build monotonically across the d5_vs_d0 and d10_vs_d5 contrasts, and whether the shared cells are stable baselines in the within-condition binomial. *Noise*: none flagged at amino-acid resolution — every cell's counts are well above the stability threshold, so no row here is discounted for low support (the codon sister's in-frame stop TGA is the first place that changes).

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is applied to a 2x2 of (aa_count, total - aa_count) at day_10 vs day_0 within each condition. `global_codon_occ_stats.py` (Analysis 3b) pools the replicate counts per (condition, timepoint) before the test and applies BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs (corrected by `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`); each of the 6 (condition, site) sub-families is corrected independently, not across the full 120-row file. The effect is reported as `log2_OR` (log2 of the CSV `odds_ratio` column): >0 = enriched at day_10 relative to day_0 within that condition, <0 = depleted. The test does **not** answer whether BWM and control move in the same direction (that BWM-vs-control comparison is the `control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction` reading) and does not test whether occupancy is enriched vs the transcriptomic background (that is the `within_condition_binomial` family).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher, so per-replicate variance is not in the test statistic — read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as extra certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (day_10 ~1.56M BWM / ~1.26M control; day_0 ~2.11M BWM / ~3.37M control), so even tiny relative deviations drive p toward 0 (4 cells underflow to exactly 0.0) — rank by `log2_OR` and `effect change`, never by p.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is applied within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs, so `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family" — compare cells within a (condition, site) slice, not across the 120-row grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the file cleanly answers "does day_10 differ from day_0 within this condition", leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be read by pairing the two arms (the divergent-direction cells below).
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** Direction-split cells (both arms meaningful, opposite signs) cluster at site A: A:N (BWM +0.250 / control -0.129), A:W (BWM -0.586 / control +0.088), A:Y (BWM -0.262 / control +0.017), A:L (BWM -0.182 / control +0.044), A:I (BWM +0.209 / control -0.017), A:C (BWM -0.046 / control +0.164) — these are the design-target reading and should anchor the BWM-specific reading at the synthesis layer.
- **near-universal-sig-large-N.** 104/120 tests clear FDR<0.05, so "both / BWM-only / control-only" section membership is weakly discriminating — rank by `|effect change|`, treating section membership as context rather than as the effect.

**Considered and ruled out**

- **rare-aa-low-count.** Every (condition, site, aa) cell has both day_10 and day_0 counts well above the `within_condition_sig_split` thresholds (BWM < 100 / control < 200); the smallest arm is BWM E:W with day_10 = 15966, so no `low-count` flag fires at AA resolution — ruled out here (the codon sister differs, where in-frame stop TGA trips it).

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM A-site amino-acid volcano: log2(OR) on x, -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05, glyphs coloured by amino-acid class. Tryptophan (W, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.59 is the far-left depleted outlier and the only point past the -0.5 guide line; asparagine (N, polar purple) and aspartate (D, acidic red) at ~= +0.25 / +0.23 are the rightmost enriched points, with isoleucine (I) and methionine (M) also enriched near +0.21. Most amino acids sit inside the +/-0.4 band; cysteine (C, purple) is the lone near-origin point close to the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM P-site amino-acid volcano: glycine (G, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.22 and lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.12 are the leftmost depleted points; glutamate (E, acidic red) ~= +0.21 leads the enriched side, with leucine (L, green) and cysteine (C, purple) modestly enriched. Most residues collapse into a tight cluster near the origin, with histidine (H), alanine (A), valine (V) and tryptophan (W) at or below the FDR line; this is the sparsest BWM panel by spread.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM E-site amino-acid volcano: proline (P, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.39 is the far-left depleted outlier and lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.13 is also depleted; serine (S, polar purple) ~= +0.25 is the rightmost enriched point, with methionine (M, green) and histidine (H, blue) enriched near +0.13. Arginine (R) and asparagine (N) sit close to the origin at the FDR line.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control A-site amino-acid volcano: glutamine (Q, polar purple) ~= -0.18, lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.15 and asparagine (N, polar purple) ~= -0.13 are the leftmost depleted points, all high on the y-axis; cysteine (C, polar purple) ~= +0.16 is the rightmost enriched point, with glutamate (E, acidic red) and phenylalanine (F, green) also enriched. Cells fall within a tighter +/-0.2 band than the BWM A-site panel, with no far-left outlier.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control P-site amino-acid volcano: aspartate (D, acidic red) ~= +0.22 and cysteine (C, polar purple) ~= +0.18 are the rightmost enriched points; lysine (K, basic blue) ~= -0.17 and isoleucine (I, hydrophobic green) ~= -0.14 are the leftmost depleted, with glutamate (E), asparagine (N) and arginine (R) also enriched. This is the most-populated control panel after the A site.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control E-site amino-acid volcano: a near-null panel — glycine (G, neutral orange) ~= -0.13 is the largest depletion and serine (S, polar purple) ~= +0.12 the largest enrichment, both modest; proline (P) and lysine (K) are depleted, leucine (L) and aspartate (D) enriched. All cells sit within a narrow +/-0.15 band, the flattest of the six panels.](../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
